ddls:
CREATE TABLE "packets" (
  "packet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each packet */,
  "packet_index" INTEGER /* Index of the packet within the session */,
  "arrival_time" REAL /* Time of packet arrival at the destination */,
  "generating_time" REAL /* Time the packet was generated */,
  "flow_delay" REAL /* Delay experienced by the packet during transmission */,
  "multicast_idx" INTEGER /* Multicast index of the packet */,
  "gw_delay" INTEGER /* Gateway delay for the packet */,
  "ue_delay" INTEGER /* User equipment delay for the packet */,
  "protocol" TEXT /* Protocol used for packet transmission */,
  "node_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the node where the packet was transmitted */,
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the session the packet belongs to */,
  "packet_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the packet type */,
  "packet_size" INTEGER /* Size of the packet in bytes */,
  "packet_priority" INTEGER /* Priority of the packet (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("packet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_packets_session_id FOREIGN KEY ("session_id") REFERENCES sessions ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_packets_node_id FOREIGN KEY ("node_id") REFERENCES nodes ("node_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_packets_protocol FOREIGN KEY ("protocol") REFERENCES protocols ("protocol_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_packets_packet_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("packet_type_id") REFERENCES packet_types ("packet_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "nodes" (
  "node_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each node */,
  "node_name" TEXT /* Name of the node */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the node */,
  "node_type" TEXT /* Type of the node (e.g., Gateway, Base Station) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the node (e.g., Active, Inactive) */,
  "node_description" TEXT /* Description of the node */,
  "node_capacity" INTEGER /* Capacity of the node (e.g., number of concurrent sessions) */,
  "node_bandwidth" INTEGER /* Bandwidth of the node (e.g., in Mbps) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("node_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sessions" (
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each session */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the session */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the session */,
  "session_type" TEXT /* Type of the session (e.g., Data, Voice) */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user initiating the session */,
  "session_description" TEXT /* Description of the session */,
  "session_status" TEXT /* Status of the session (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sessions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., network engineer, data analyst, admin) */,
  "user_description" TEXT /* Description of the user */,
  "user_status" TEXT /* Status of the user (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "protocols" (
  "protocol_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each protocol */,
  "protocol_name" TEXT /* Name of the protocol */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the protocol */,
  "protocol_version" TEXT /* Version of the protocol */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("protocol_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "packet_types" (
  "packet_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each packet type */,
  "packet_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the packet type (e.g., Data, Control) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the packet type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("packet_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "node_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "node_id" INTEGER /* ID of the node the log entry is associated with */,
  "log_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the log entry */,
  "log_type" TEXT /* Type of the log entry (e.g., Status Change, Error) */,
  "log_message" TEXT /* Message of the log entry */,
  "log_level" TEXT /* Level of the log entry (e.g., debug, info, warn, error) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_node_logs_node_id FOREIGN KEY ("node_id") REFERENCES nodes ("node_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "session_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "session_id" INTEGER /* ID of the session the log entry is associated with */,
  "log_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the log entry */,
  "log_type" TEXT /* Type of the log entry (e.g., Start, End, Error) */,
  "log_message" TEXT /* Message of the log entry */,
  "log_level" TEXT /* Level of the log entry (e.g., debug, info, warn, error) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_session_logs_session_id FOREIGN KEY ("session_id") REFERENCES sessions ("session_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "session_id" INTEGER /* ID of the session the metric is associated with */,
  "node_id" INTEGER /* ID of the node the metric is associated with */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the performance metric (e.g., Average Delay, Packet Loss) */,
  "metric_value" REAL /* Value of the performance metric */,
  "metric_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the metric was recorded */,
  "metric_description" TEXT /* Description of the performance metric */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_node_id FOREIGN KEY ("node_id") REFERENCES nodes ("node_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_session_id FOREIGN KEY ("session_id") REFERENCES sessions ("session_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., network engineer, data analyst, admin) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role the permission is associated with */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_sessions" (
  "user_session_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user session */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who initiated the session */,
  "session_id" INTEGER /* ID of the session */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the session */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the session */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_sessions_session_id FOREIGN KEY ("session_id") REFERENCES sessions ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_sessions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
